disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Excessive water intake (over 3 litres in a few hours) driven by fear of high blood sugar, with resulting frequent urination
trigger_question_examples: How much have you had to drink tonight?
What started the thirst and the frequent urination?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've drunk more than 3 litres of water in the last few hours trying to flush the sugar out because I panicked, and now I can't stop going to the toilet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known slow-progressing adult-onset autoimmune diabetes, recently reclassified, managed on diet and an oral agent, not on insulin
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me about your diabetes and how it's managed?
Are you on any diabetes medications or insulin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My diabetes came on slowly as an adult and my doctor recently said it's actually the autoimmune type — Type 1 developing, with antibodies. I take metformin and manage with diet; I'm not on insulin.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vomiting and no abdominal pain
trigger_question_examples: Have you been vomiting at all?
Do you have any tummy or abdominal pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't thrown up at all and my tummy feels fine — no pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fruity/acetone smell on the breath and breathing feels comfortable and unlaboured
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone noticed a sweet or fruity smell on your breath?
Is your breathing fast, deep or laboured?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fruity smell that I've noticed, and my breathing feels totally normal — I'm not puffing or gasping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Fully alert and oriented, not drowsy or confused
trigger_question_examples: Are you feeling drowsy, sleepy or muddled at all?
Can you think clearly right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm wide awake and thinking clearly — if anything I'm too wired to sleep.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intense fear of a diabetic emergency and of eventually needing insulin, which is driving the behaviour
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling in yourself about all this?
What are you most worried might happen tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm terrified — since they told me it's the autoimmune type I keep thinking I'll slip into a coma overnight, and the thought of needing insulin scares me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recent dietary indiscretion this evening preceding the symptoms
trigger_question_examples: Did anything happen with food or drink before this started?
Was there anything different about your eating this evening?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I had a bit of a slip-up with my eating this evening and that's when I panicked about my sugar.
